summary: Of the 10 problems in first_proof_1, seven (q1, q2, q3, q7, q8, q9, q10) have fully resolved their single tracked proof issue, indicating completed or near-complete proofs. The remaining activity is concentrated in q4, q5, and q6, which carry the bulk of open issues; q6 is nearly closed with only 1 open issue, while q4 (22 open) and q5 (14 open) require the most attention.
problems: q4 has 22 open issues, the highest in the dataset, including a limiting argument from simple to general roots, a Lemma 1 implicit differentiation proof, and a dimensional error (JJ* vs J*J) in the Hessian.
q5 has 14 open issues spanning a connectivity converse argument, well-definedness of chi^O(H) as a minimum, and a 'discrete slices' conclusion that does not follow.
q6 has 1 remaining open issue: a missing rank argument that the top-sigma bar holds.
Open issues across q4-q6 cluster around rigor gaps: dimensional/linear-algebra errors, rank arguments, well-definedness, and limiting/generalization steps.
highlights: Seven of ten problems (q1, q2, q3, q7, q8, q9, q10) have zero open issues and a resolved proof, suggesting completed work.
q6 has strong traction with 15 resolved versus only 1 open issue and is likely the next problem to fully close.
q4 shows high engagement with 23 resolved issues, indicating active and productive progress despite remaining open items.

suggested_todos.title: Close out q6 by completing the missing rank argument for the top-sigma bar
Resolve the dimensional error (JJ* vs J*J) in the q4 Hessian, a likely tractable linear-algebra fix
Triage q4's 22 open issues to identify quick wins versus structural blockers
Address well-definedness of chi^O(H) as a minimum and the 'discrete slices' gap in q5
Verify the limiting argument from simple roots to general roots in q4
Confirm and archive the seven resolved problems (q1, q2, q3, q7, q8, q9, q10) as completed
